title: BEAM Therapeutics Secures $500 Million Sixth Street Financing
text: Beam Therapeutics secured a $500 million senior secured credit facility from Sixth Street, comprising $100 million funded at closing, up to $300 million available upon achievement of clinical, regulatory, and commercial milestones for risto-cel, and an additional $100 million available at Beam's discretion with mutual agreement. The facility matures in early 2033 with an annual interest rate of approximately 10% based on current SOFR projections. This strategic financing provides Beam with significant flexibility and long-term, non-dilutive capital for commercializing their sickle cell disease treatment candidate. Key features include no scheduled amortization with principal due at maturity, prepayment allowed with customary yield maintenance fees, minimum $200 million draw expected, and secured by first-priority liens on substantially all Beam assets. The capital will support the anticipated commercial launch of ristoglogene autogetemcel (risto-cel) and enable Beam to direct resources toward pipeline expansion and precision genetic medicine development.
